w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring multiple NVIDIA A100 GPUs for demanding AI/ML workloads, balancing strong GPU performance with moderate CPU capabilities.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g: Accelerator-optimized with 4 NVIDIA A100 GPUs and 48 x86_64 vCPUs for intensive AI/ML tasks.
c200: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g offers 4 NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs with 160GB VRAM, 48 x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 340GB RAM. Designed for high-throughput machine learning and data science.
w150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-4g is an accelerator-optimized instance designed for intensive parallel processing. It features an x86_64 architecture, specifically Intel Xeon processors, providing 48 virtual CPUs across 24 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz. The CPU includes 38 MB of L3 cache and supports AVX512F and AVX512_VNNI instructions. This is complemented by 340 GB of system memory, offering an average memory bandwidth for both small and large block read/write operations. The server is equipped with four NVIDIA Tesla A100 GPUs, collectively delivering 160 GB of total VRAM, with no local storage bundled. While single-core CPU performance, as indicated by stress-ng and Geekbench scores, falls into the bottom 25%, multi-core CPU performance and general memory bandwidth are categorized as average. Crucially, LLM inference speeds for prompt processing and text generation using 70B models demonstrate top-tier performance. This configuration is primarily suited for demanding data science jobs, machine learning model training, large language model inference, and other GPU-accelerated computing tasks where parallel processing capabilities are paramount.
